goal: recover all controls, page transitions, and logic behavior on the current target screen

proven_foundations.summary: the exact data-record plus text-select case80-aligned scene now closes the loop end to end: scene build reproduces the official no-event data0+select0 oracle byte-for-byte and the result still passes COM36 live smoke

the exact data-record plus sliding-text case85-aligned scene now closes the loop end to end: scene build reproduces the official no-event data0+slt0 oracle byte-for-byte and the result still passes COM36 live smoke

the exact data-record plus text-select plus ordinary button case83-aligned scene now closes the loop end to end: scene build reproduces the official no-event data0+select0+b1 oracle byte-for-byte and the result still passes COM36 live smoke
the exact case83-aligned page0 shape with b1.down -> printh 23 83 42 54 is now public and has fresh COM36 runtime proof

highest_leverage_unsolved_subsystem.reason: it is the common runtime explanation layer behind page transitions, load/unload, timers, page1 advanced binding, and logic control

binding_matrix_summary.highest_leverage_gap: general scheduler/lifecycle and page-local advanced runtime registration

unfinished.scope: general page load/unload, timer, lifecycle callback binding, and scheduler equivalence
why_unfinished: Official lifecycle oracles keep scheduler_path at page_event_boundary_without_page_callback and leave callback slots 0x0c/0x10/0x14 empty.
The local generated page0 positive proof is real, but it is a narrow post_primary_page_event path and does not prove general lifecycle equivalence.
Blindly writing page mirror callback slots 0x0c/0x10/0x14 to the page1 event table start fails cleanly and does not recover page1 lifecycle dispatch.
blocks: confident expansion beyond narrow object-callback proofs

unfinished.scope: page1 advanced controls and page-local advanced object lookup/binding
why_unfinished: a local ordinary page1 load/text probe uploads and page-switches, but page1 load emits no expected marker and get p1title.txt still returns invalid_reference
local multi-page advanced probe uploads and page switches succeed, but page1 advanced object get/click paths return invalid_reference or wrong markers
official GUI-created page1 text-select, sliding-text, data-record, file-stream, and cloned file-browser still compile positive yet remain invalid_reference on COM36
blocks: page1 advanced controls as supported authoring/runtime path

unfinished.scope: arbitrary property synthesis and wider advanced-control mixing beyond the narrow proven shapes

broader advanced-control mixing and arbitrary field synthesis remain not claimed in the current target limitations

negative evidence shows fs0.down self paths still do not dispatch correctly
